status: analysis-only (read-only re-analysis of tracked M3 oracle artifact)
claim: Tie-aware evidence shows two strict failure subsets: B7-bge is below query-only on 12.53% of all requests and below history-only on 30.35%. The frozen assignment slices remain useful for conditional averages, but the 60.6% query assignment is not a strict-preference or failure rate.

over_personalization_query_optimal.interpretation: this is the frozen query-first assignment slice and includes ties; B7-bge is strictly below query-only on 18.61% of this slice (12.53% of all requests), which is the defensible over-personalization subset

under_personalization_history_optimal.interpretation: on requests where pure history is the best available channel, query-only and the static mixture both use too little history (under-personalization)

aggregate_paradox.explanation: B7-bge wins in aggregate over query-only while remaining strictly below query-only on 12.53% of all requests and strictly below history-only on 30.35%. The 60.6% query-assigned slice contains many ties and must not be described as a 60.6% loss rate. See reports/pps_m3_tie_aware_audit.json.
